labels.customImages.iconSizing: For icons (things drawn in the Icons tool), to control the size your images draw on your map, you can specify a width or height in the file name, such as width= (or w for short) or height= (or h for short). For example, an image named "large castle w28.png" will draw at 28 units wide (where a unit is a measurement based on the width of polygons in the map). Only width or height can be given, not both; that way scaling respects the aspect ratio. If you specify the size of just one image in a folder, then the others will be scaled proportionately to that one. Note that mountain and hill sizes vary based on the size of the polygons they are initially drawn on. Also, any icon can be resized on the map by editing it using the Icons tool, and the Effects tab has slider bars to resize all icons of a type.

labels.subMapDialog.step2.polygons.tooltip:
The number of Voronoi polygons in the sub-map, which controls its level of detail.
The multiplier shows how many times more polygons the sub-map has relative
to the equivalent area of the source map. Values below 1× mean less detail.
Values above 1× mean more detail. The number of polygons must be between {0}
and {1}.

labels.keyboardShortcuts.message:
Keyboard shortcuts for navigating the map:
  • Zoom: Mouse wheel
  • Pan: Hold mouse middle button, or Shift and mouse left click, then drag

Each editor tool has a keyboard shortcut for switching to it. Hover over the tool's icon to see the shortcut.
labels.theme.texture.help: Select a texture image as a seed from installed images that came with Nortantis, or from art packs or a custom images folder.
